articles.title: Reconcile before you round: lessons from a 14M-row open data study
articles.excerpt: We recently published a data study on the French business registry — 14.4 million active establishments, sliced by workforce, sector and geography. The numbers themselves aren't the interesting part f…
articles.body: We recently published a data study on the French business registry — 14.4 million active establishments, sliced by workforce, sector and geography. The numbers themselves aren't the interesting part for this post. What is: the rules we forced ourselves to follow so that anyone re-running the same queries on the same open dataset would get the exact same numbers , and so every headline percentage could be traced back to a total someone else can check. This is a walkthrough of that process, using INSEE's SIRENE registry (France's official open-data business registry) as the example dataset, because it's public, it's messy in the ways real government data tends to be, and the mistakes we ran into are the boring, common kind — not exotic edge cases.
articles.author: OutSend

articles.title: Ultrahuman’s new ‘Emerald’ update is a massive overhaul of how the app works
articles.excerpt: You can now pair your Apple Watch, Garmin, or WHOOP with the Ultrahuman app.
articles.body: TL;DR Ultrahuman is rolling out a new “Emerald” update for its companion app. It introduces a new “UltraSphere” decision-making engine that suggests “Next Best Actions” to users. It also offers better health insights and new tabs such as “Sleep Screener,” “Longevity,” and “Windows.” Ultrahuman, the company behind fitness rings like the Ultrahuman Ring PRO , today announced a major “Emerald” update to the Ultrahuman app, introducing a fresh design and new features to help users get the most out of their fitness trackers. The update aims to make health insights easier to understand so users can make better health decisions. At the core of the new experience is the “UltraSphere” — a decision engine that lives on the home screen and offers “Next Best Actions” to users.
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar

articles.title: Just for Fun: Fear and Loathing in the Age of Coding Agents
articles.excerpt: There is a distinct, quiet disquiet settling over the software engineering landscape today. You can feel it in LinkedIn post threads, in hushed conversations over coffee with senior colleagues, and in…
articles.body: There is a distinct, quiet disquiet settling over the software engineering landscape today. You can feel it in LinkedIn post threads, in hushed conversations over coffee with senior colleagues, and in the background noise of tech news cycles. On one side of the coin, we are navigating an unprecedented era of tech layoffs, budget contractions, and corporate belt-tightening. On the other side, artificial intelligence is advancing at a dizzying, almost relentless speed. Coding agents write entire pull requests, generate complex unit tests, and spin up functional microservices in seconds. It is almost impossible, even for the most seasoned engineer, not to feel a creeping sense of existential dread. Will things go sideways for my career?
articles.author: Remo H. Jansen

articles.title: Local Kubernetes Dev — Part 2: Production-like environments — what and why
articles.excerpt: "But I've got a docker-compose.yaml, it spins up my service with a database — why would I run Kubernetes locally?" This is the most common beginner misconception, and it's an expensive one. A Compose…
articles.body: "But I've got a docker-compose.yaml, it spins up my service with a database — why would I run Kubernetes locally?" This is the most common beginner misconception, and it's an expensive one. A Compose file is structurally NOT equivalent to Kubernetes manifests, and "works in compose" guarantees nothing about "works in k8s." Just look at the official Kompose converter, whose authors honestly warn you: depends_on is ignored, build: won't build an image, bind-mounts don't carry over, and the output is manifests with NO requests/limits, NO probes, and environment variables in plain text instead of a Secret. In other words, exactly the production-like properties we spin up a local cluster for are the ones Compose skips. Part two of the local Kubernetes series is the philosophical one.
articles.author: Mikhail Dorokhovich

articles.title: Performance and Efficiency
articles.excerpt: Performance matters Performance is a quality dimension viewers will notice without measuring anything [1] [3]. A viewer presses the remote, the splash screen stays on screen for a little longer than d…
articles.body: Performance matters Performance is a quality dimension viewers will notice without measuring anything [1] [3] . A viewer presses the remote, the splash screen stays on screen for a little longer than desired, and they're already deciding whether the app is slow and unusable. A slow launch, a stuttering menu, or a stalled scroll pushes viewers to uninstall and not return. Strong teams treat Performance and Efficiency as ongoing work, not a periodic sprint, and bake it into how every change is reviewed before it ships. Performance drives all product features of the application, strong content, thoughtful design, and personalized recommendations cannot survive a blank launch screen or a UI that drops frames while navigating.
articles.author: Ashutosh Joglekar

articles.title: Insight and Telemetry
articles.excerpt: This pillar sets out the telemetry a streaming app needs to detect quality problems, trace them to a cause, and turn them into engineering decisions. Insight and Telemetry is the practice of making th…
articles.body: This pillar sets out the telemetry a streaming app needs to detect quality problems, trace them to a cause, and turn them into engineering decisions. Insight and Telemetry is the practice of making the viewer experience visible, measurable, and actionable across every stage of a playback session. Teams can improve performance, stability, and streaming quality through architecture decisions, testing, and device-farm validation. Most check releases against aggregate metrics like crash rate and playback start success. But aggregate metrics hide failures that concentrate on one device family, OS version, or marketplace. Staged rollouts cannot show where in the viewing session the experience broke.
articles.author: Kostakis Bouzouaks

articles.title: High-Quality Streaming Apps on Amazon Fire TV: Introducing the Blueprint
articles.excerpt: When viewers open a streaming app on Amazon Fire TV, they expect it to start quickly, be easy to navigate with a remote, and begin playback reliably. In a living-room environment, quality issues are v…
articles.body: When viewers open a streaming app on Amazon Fire TV, they expect it to start quickly, be easy to navigate with a remote, and begin playback reliably. In a living-room environment, quality issues are visible immediately. If the app is slow, confusing, or unreliable, viewers may lose patience and leave, even when the content itself is strong. Why teams need the same quality view This post introduces the Fire TV Streaming App Quality Blueprint, a six-part series of posts for partner and app teams responsible for streaming experiences on Fire TV. Each post will cover what teams should have in place, why it matters for viewers and for the teams responsible for the app, and the questions teams and leaders should ask during quality reviews.
articles.author: Anisha Malde

articles.title: Microsoft talks LG into removing McAfee adware from its… monitors
articles.excerpt: LG's automatically downloaded monitor app will no longer show McAfee pop-ups, but another major concern remains.
articles.body: Credit: Jonathan Feist / Android Authority TL;DR A Microsoft executive has confirmed that LG has agreed to disable McAfee pop-ups on their monitor app. This comes after it emerged that LG was automatically installing bloatware when you connected its monitors to your PC. This doesn’t solve the problem of bloat being installed on your PC without your consent, though. LG came under fire earlier this week after it emerged that it was automatically installing bloatware when you connected its monitors to your PC. What made matters infinitely worse was that the app in question displayed McAfee pop-ups on your desktop whenever you started up your computer. Now, Microsoft executive Pavan Davuluri has announced on X that LG has agreed to disable the McAfee pop-up in their app.
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: Gemini’s glowy makeover appears headed for the floating overlay and Live screens
articles.excerpt: Gemini is trying to love more alive than ever.
articles.body: Credit: Adamya Sharma / Android Authority TL;DR Google recently introduced the neural design for its Gemini launcher on Android, and it’s now being tested across other parts, including the floating overlay. These elements include a flowy overlay rather than a solid shape, with colorful dots sprinkled in the background. The redesign also brings new buttons for the Live interface, with a similarly flowy design. Around two months ago, Google introduced Gemini’s new neural design with 3D visual effects and spatial animations , almost gesturing at its transformation for the upcoming Android XR visual interface. The effects were largely limited to Gemini’s homescreen on Android, as well as regular (non-Live) chats.
articles.author: Tushar Mehta

articles.title: Survey says Google Tensor has reached the end of the road, but some Pixel fans disagree
articles.excerpt: After nearly five years of Tensor, most Pixel fans say Google’s custom chips still aren’t cutting it.
articles.body: It’s been nearly five years since Google launched its Tensor SoC line to take charge of its AI and machine learning priorities on Pixel phones. In certain aspects, the Tensor line has delivered on this goal, but it has also failed in other areas that matter. Battery management, heat output, and waning performance have left many users disillusioned with Google’s silicon. And, as my colleague Stephen Radochia explains , even the AI ambitions fall short for users of older yet still capable phones.
articles.author: Andy Walker

articles.title: NodeBB Patches Eight AI-Found Flaws Exposing Admin Access and Private Chats
articles.excerpt: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a six-hour r…
articles.body: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a six-hour review of the forum software's source code. Every version before 4.14.0 is affected. NodeBB has fixed them all, and administrators should be on 4.14.2. The simplest one takes a settings change. A

articles.title: The Galaxy Z Fold 8 series borrows one of the best foldable multitasking ideas
articles.excerpt: You can now do a 90:10 split with three apps on Samsung's new foldables.
articles.body: Credit: Sanuj Bhatia / Android Authority TL;DR One UI 9 introduces a new Open Canvas-style 90:10 three-app split-screen layout on the Galaxy Z Fold 8 series. The wider Galaxy Z Fold 8 can arrange three apps side by side vertically, while the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is limited to one large app alongside two stacked apps. Samsung’s own documentation confirms that the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra supports new two-way and three-way split views, but it doesn’t detail the layouts. Samsung spent plenty of time talking about Galaxy AI, bigger batteries, and thinner hardware during its latest Galaxy Unpacked event, but one of the most interesting improvements to the Galaxy Z Fold 8 series may have flown under the radar.

The Streams chains also need EVAL and XGROUP; the 8.8.0 chain needs EVAL and the bundled RedisBloom module. Redis says the underlying memory flaws may lead to remote code execution. articles.title: Fool us three times? Galaxy Watch owners are reporting abnormal battery drain yet again
articles.excerpt: It's been a horrible year for battery life if you're a Galaxy Watch owner.
articles.body: Credit: Tushar Mehta / Android Authority TL;DR Some Galaxy Watch owners are reporting major battery drain issues. The problem seems to affect multiple models, including the Galaxy Watch 6, 7, and 8 lines. This is at least the third time this year that a flood of Galaxy Watch owners have reported abnormal battery drain. Samsung has just launched the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 , but it’s been a topsy-turvy year if you own an older Galaxy Watch. These users reported battery drain issues in 2026, and it sounds like the problem has struck again. Multiple Reddit users have reported major battery drain problems on their Samsung smartwatches. The problem seems to affect a variety of devices, including the Galaxy Watch 8, Galaxy Watch 7, and Galaxy Watch 6 generations.

articles.excerpt: A selection of episodes from Seth Green’s animated Star Wars sitcom will be exhibited at the soon-to-open Lucas Museum.
articles.body: Seth Green’s long-shelved animated Star Wars sitcom Star Wars: Detours will finally find itself in front of the public, albeit as an exhibit at George Lucas’ soon-to-open Lucas Museum of Narrative Art in Los Angeles, California. The news comes via an announcement from Green himself during a panel discussion on the museum at San Diego Comic-Con 2026, as reported by Entertainment Weekly. “We made a show with George, an animated series,” said Green. “He wanted to make Simpsons in the Star Wars universe.

articles.title: 'It's Being Written, Is the Short Answer' — Zach Cregger Gives an Update on the Aunt Gladys Prequel | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Director Zach Cregger said that the Aunt Gladys-focused Weapons prequel movie is in the works and will have "lots of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things" at San Diego Comic-Con.
articles.body: Weapons and upcoming Resident Evil director Zach Cregger gave an update on the status of his Aunt Gladys prequel film at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday afternoon. "First of all, I'm really excited about the Gladys movie. I loved working with Amy [Madigan], I love that character," Cregger said. "For the movie Weapons, it really made a lot of sense not to dive into her backstory because the device of Weapons was to keep her very opaque and very mysterious. However, I think there's an amazing story to be told about where Gladys comes from and how she became what we know her as. It's got a lot of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things, kind of like Weapons does, and I think it's gonna be great.
articles.author: Leanne Butkovic

articles.excerpt: All the big announcements out of the Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender panel at San Diego Comic-Con 2026.
articles.body: While showing off an early concept photo of the aged-up Team Avatar at a San Diego Comic-Con panel on Thursday afternoon, Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender director Lauren Montgomery recalled one of the films' earliest leaks in a rollout that's been plagued by unfortunate leaks . The image in question was what Montgomery called the "Avengers shot," with the characters posing together that leaked out of CinemaCon in 2024 before it was taken down. "This was one of the very first funny stories about this image. We had a version of this image, exact same painting. The characters looked a little bit different," Montgomery said. "Our production designer Jake [Panian] was never really happy with it.

articles.title: This is the new 18-in-1 electric vehicle you can build yourself
articles.excerpt: If you’ve ever wished you could buy one electric vehicle that could evolve with your needs instead of replacing it every time your use case changes, a Canadian company thinks it has the answer. ENVO D…
articles.body: If you’ve ever wished you could buy one electric vehicle that could evolve with your needs instead of replacing it every time your use case changes, a Canadian company thinks it has the answer. ENVO Drive Systems has unveiled its new Utility Personal Transporter (UPT), a modular electric vehicle platform that can be configured into as many as 18 different vehicle types, with even more combinations possible through optional accessories and upgrades.
articles.author: Micah Toll

articles.title: Motor City's director explains how he made the most unique revenge thriller of the year
articles.excerpt: The filmmaker discusses Alan Ritchson, visual storytelling, unforgettable needle drops, and why "the first person to speak usually loses."
articles.body: After spending nearly two decades in development, Motor City has finally reached the big screen. Starring Alan Ritchson as an ex-Army Ranger seeking revenge after being framed and sent to prison, the film tells much of its story with little dialogue, relying instead on physical performances, visual storytelling, and a soundtrack of classic rock needle drops that become as important as the characters themselves.
articles.author: Terry Terrones

articles.title: League of Legends Riftbound TCG: First Impressions of the Vendetta Booster Set | IGN Preview
articles.excerpt: Get an early look at League of Legends Riftbound's upcoming Vendetta expansion.
articles.body: The wait is finally over for Riftbound’s Vendetta booster set, as its release is now just around the corner. This will be the League of Legends TCG’s fourth big drop with pre-release events at local stores starting July 24th with the global release on July 31st. That’s right! With the game having about a year under its belt now, fans finally have a synced-up release with both English and Chinese languages for the first time. Not only is this great for the meta, but players will also have some new deck combinations to mess around with as the set introduces new legends with opposing colors or domains and, of course, new mechanics like Flow to give some extra options out of hand.
articles.author: Mike Mamon

articles.title: House of the Dragon Larys Strong Actor Matthew Needham Voices Joker in Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Matthew Needham is the voice of The Joker in Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2, Prime Video has announced. British actor Needham is most famous for playing Larys Strong in Game of Thrones prequel series…
articles.body: Matthew Needham is the voice of The Joker in Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2, Prime Video has announced. British actor Needham is most famous for playing Larys Strong in Game of Thrones prequel series House of the Dragon. Confirmation comes from the Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 panel at San Diego Comic-Con, which ended with a trailer for Amazon’s upcoming Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles game . It’s exclusive to Amazon Luna (and now, part of Amazon Prime ), and launches alongside all 10 episodes of Season 2 on July 31. Batman: Caped Crusader is a superhero noir animated series created by Bruce Timm (co-creator of Batman: The Animated Series), The Batman director Matt Reeves, and J.J. Abrams.
articles.author: Wesley Yin-Poole

articles.title: Amazon Prime Has an Exclusive Batman Video Game Set to Launch Alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Amazon and Warner Bros. have announced Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles, a new Batman game exclusive to Amazon Prime set to launch on July 31 alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2.
articles.body: Amazon and Warner Bros. have announced Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles, a new Batman game exclusive to Amazon Prime set to launch on July 31 alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 . Both the game and the new season of the animated show will be available for Amazon Prime members at no additional cost. The game is part of Amazon Luna’s move into Prime Video, which launched today ahead of the Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 panel at San Diego Comic-Con. Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles is a cooperative crime-solving party game for up to four players. The idea is that people will watch Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 then play the game, using their phones as controllers.
articles.author: Wesley Yin-Poole

articles.title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
articles.excerpt: NASA's SunRISE mission has been assigned a new launch vehicle: It will now be delivered to orbit by a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket rather than a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur.
articles.body: NASA is shaking things up for its next mission to research the sun. The six satellites of NASA's Sun Radio Interferometer Space Experiment (SunRISE) mission are poised to launch to Earth orbit soon, to serve as an early warning system for incoming solar storms. They were originally slated to lift off on a United Launch Alliance (ULA) Vulcan Centaur rocket but have now been assigned a SpaceX Falcon Heavy , according to a NASA mission update . SunRISE will fly to space as part of a rideshare mission sponsored by the U.S. Space Force's (USSF's) Space Systems Command (SSC). Though it's not stated specifically in the NASA release, that may be a hint as to why the mission's rocket was changed.
articles.author: Josh Dinner

articles.title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
articles.excerpt: 'We've seen a lot of strange new worlds, but we’ll never see something like this again'
articles.body: 2026 is the 60th anniversary of "Star Trek". To help celebrate the show that creator Gene Roddenberry described as "Wagon Train to the Stars,""Star Trek: Strange New Worlds" returns to Paramount+ for its penultimate season. From teasers and trailers that first dropped last summer and fall, we’re anticipating a rollicking batch of dynamic episodes that draw on diverse subgenres, with everything from classic film noir crime stories and time travel to spooky ghost stories and sword-clashing swashbucklers. But the end is nigh for this colorful modern interpretation of "Trek". Next year's truncated fifth season will be the crew's final mission, so let's revel in the time we have left and beam onto the Enterprise bridge for a promising round of missions.

articles.title: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of the sun's missing silver
articles.excerpt: Scientists say the sun has a little more silver than we thought.
articles.body: For several years now, astronomers who study the sun have faced a little mystery — our star seemed to hold too little silver. Whenever scientists examined the sun's outer layers, they've seen significantly less silver than they've expected to find. Where, then, did the missing silver go? As it turns out, this mystery may finally be solved. Newly published research suggests that the sun 's missing silver may have been hiding in plain sight all along. At first, the real mystery might seem to be why you'd seek silver in the sun at all. After all, 98.5% of the sun's mass is made from lightweight hydrogen and helium. Silver is just a tiny fraction of the remaining 1.5%, which also includes traces of other heavy elements like iron and copper.

articles.title: How much better is the Canon EOS R6 III than previous models for astrophotography?
articles.excerpt: The latest edition of the Canon EOS R6 III adds 8 MP in resolution compared to the EOS R6 II, a CFexpress card slot and improved low-light autofocusing capability but do these upgrades actually benefi…
articles.body: In a rare first for me, I actually used autofocus for this entire astrophotography shoot. If I'm being honest? I prefer it when shooting astro with a mirrorless camera . Why? Because it is quicker and that means more time capturing the stars and less time setting up. I can get a star in sharp focus using autofocus in half the time that it would take me to do it manually and it's way easier to focus with the shutter or back button than play about with the focus ring, especially with cold fingers. The Canon EOS R6 III makes shooting astro much quicker with its rapid low-light autofocus.
